expectations.text: Output contains an audit report table with columns: #, Issue, Severity, Location, Fix
expectations.evidence: Severity Table at line 68 has exact header: '| # | Issue | Severity | Location | Fix |' with 14 rows covering all findings.

expectations.text: Report checks all 5 dimensions: dependencies, modernity, performance, conflict, consistency
expectations.evidence: Dimension Analysis section contains subsections for all 5: '1. DEPENDENCIES' (line 12), '2. MODERNITY' (line 28), '3. PERFORMANCE' (line 47), '4. CONFLICT' (line 55), '5. CONSISTENCY' (line 61).

expectations.text: CRITICAL issues are identified (e.g. no GSAP loaded, no scroll animations, no reduced-motion guard)
expectations.evidence: 5 CRITICAL issues identified: #1 'No GSAP library loaded', #2 'No hero entrance timeline', #3 'No scroll-triggered reveals on any section', #4 'No prefers-reduced-motion guard', #5 'Custom cursor elements exist in DOM but have no JS'.

expectations.text: Summary line states count of CRITICAL, WARNING, and INFO items
expectations.evidence: Line 85: '**Summary:** 5 critical, 3 warnings, 6 info' -- counts match actual rows in the severity table (issues 1-5 CRITICAL, 6-8 WARNING, 9-14 INFO).

expectations.text: Concrete 'Before/After' fix patches are provided for CRITICAL and WARNING items
expectations.evidence: Fixes #1-#8 each contain explicit Before/After code blocks with copy-paste-ready HTML/JS patches. All 5 CRITICAL fixes (#1-#5) and all 3 WARNING fixes (#6-#8) have concrete code patches.

Every fix section (#1 through #8) ends with a '**Why:**' paragraph explaining the rationale. Examples: Fix #1 'The page cannot animate anything without GSAP loaded', Fix #4 'WCAG 2.1 SC 2.3.3 requires respecting it', Fix #7 'CSS scroll-behavior: smooth interferes with ScrollTrigger scroll position calculations'.

expectations.text: Report identifies that the page has NO animation code despite having animation-related classes
expectations.evidence: Multiple explicit callouts: Dependencies section states 'No GSAP CDN loaded, No CSS animation or transition declarations, No inline JavaScript -- the page talks extensively about GSAP patterns in its marketing copy but does not actually implement any of them'. Overall Assessment: 'fully static HTML document with zero animation implementation' with 'well-structured class names that map directly to animation patterns (.hero-badge, .magnetic-btn, .card, .process-step, .scroll-line, .marquee-track) but none of these are wired to any animation logic'.
